replies.author: @avarion9538
replies.text:
same. The thing is, usually Disney has contracts in place. So they tell the theaters, they have to use a certain amount of screens for their movies. So even when 1 screen per theater would be enough, or even when it's empty, they have to show it due to the contract, until that expires.

The Theaters might get some money from Disney to show the movie, but I doubt it. I can imagine, it's something like, either the theater shows it, or it won't be allowed to show other Disney movies in future.

I don't know which of the two is the case, or if they have a different contract, but yes, it's VERY bad for the theaters.

author: @spacecadet35
text: Taking into account the average ticket price in the US, that is three tickets per screen for the weekend. Allow four tickets per screen as children's tickets are cheaper. People are likely to see a movie on Mother's Day in pairs.  So that is one pair per screening per day. And the theatre is probably running four+ screenings a day. That means, best case scenario,  at least 75% of all screenings had no paying customers in the theatre.
